canvas.webp.title: Canvas
canvas.webp.description: I brought a small canvas to every musicshow I went to. I asked the artists & befriended artists to draw something on it. This formed a collection drawings from artists like [Gus Dapperton](https://open.spotify.com/artist/6sHCvZe1PHrOAuYlwTLNH4), [Tennyson](https://open.spotify.com/artist/3Nb8N20WChM0swo5qWTvm8), [PointPoint](https://open.spotify.com/artist/5L67d6oZKOOmXcBcMMJdZZ), [Droeloe](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0u18Cq5stIQLUoIaULzDmA), [Metsä](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4e6vHsG1DOc9ei7zar3ds7), [San Holo](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0jNDKefhfSbLR9sFvcPLHo), etc. etc.

Summerland1.webp.title: SoundPatrol @Summerland Festival
Summerland1.webp.description: One of the bigger shows I played as SoundPatrol in the summer of 2018 at Summerland Festival, near Liège, Belgium. I played a 1 hour set on the main stage, which was a big deal for me at the time. I had been playing small shows for a few years, but this was my first big festival. Especially since it was alongside my friends [Droeloe](https://soundcloud.com/droeloemusic), [LIHO](https://soundcloud.com/itsliho), [Erkaï](https://soundcloud.com/bonjour_matisse) and [SVDKO](https://soundcloud.com/sadkomusic).

Luisterns webp.webp.title: Luister'NS
Luisterns webp.webp.description:
I wrote and illustrated a book filled with conversations I overheard while taking the train. 

The book is in Dutch, but the title translates to 'Listen'NS', which is a play on the Dutch word for 'listen' (luister) and the name of the Dutch railway company (NS). 
I want to thank NS for the materials and their juridical insights.

There is also a somewhat cringy interview with me about the book, which you can read [here](https://www.gelderlander.nl/mijndg/tim-observeerde-uren-en-uren-gesprekken-in-de-trein-heel-oppervlakkige-praatjes~ac2406d3/?slug_rd=1).

Speld.webp.title: De Speld
Speld.webp.description: Branded content for the Dutch satirical news website [De Speld](https://www.speld.nl/). I can't show you all of them, but here are a few examples. [Ikea](https://speld.nl/2020/08/28/leuke-keukenspullen-om-kwijt-te-raken-in-je-gezamenlijke-studentenkeuken/), [KPN](https://speld.nl/2020/03/04/man-durft-date-niet-mee-naar-huis-te-nemen-uit-schaamte-voor-zijn-slappe-wifi/), [Milieudefensie](https://speld.nl/category/campagne/milieudefensie/), [Trimbos](https://speld.nl/2020/11/17/hert-niet-enthousiast-over-xtc-trip/), [Beefeater](https://speld.nl/2018/04/20/glas-gin-tonic-operatief-verwijderd-uit-hand-van-vrouw/)

Paper Crane 3.webp.title: Paper Crane
Paper Crane 3.webp.description:
Paper Crane was a LA-based music collective started in 2015 by [Kyle](https://soundcloud.com/madnap) and me consisting for artists such as [Omniboi](https://soundcloud.com/omniboi), [Diamond Eyes](https://soundcloud.com/Iamdiamondeyes) & [DZZ](https://soundcloud.com/diamonds4alex). 
All of us met on Soundcloud and in the course of 2 years we released 4 albums and had some collaborative offshoots such as [WWW](https://soundcloud.com/w3col). We also played a [few shows](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3XgjiFz9o88) over the summer of 2017 in NY and LA wich I joined, with some help of our friends over at [NON STOP POP](https://www.nts.live/shows/jack) and [Brownies & Lemonade](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3XgjiFz9o88).

Artworks by [Princeling](https://princeling.net/)

DEMO2.webp.title: Democrazy
DEMO2.webp.description:
I did my MA internship at [Democrazy](https://www.democrazy.be/), a Belgian concert venue based in Ghent. I was responsible for their communication, social media and newsletter. 

Apart from that, I did research into the question 'who is our audience?' and also created a tool for not only tracking it, but also predicting how a certain artist will click with the audience. 

The tool is based on a combination of Spotify data and their own ticketing data, and it uses machine learning to make predictions. I also created a dashboard for the team to easily access the data and insights. This informs both the marketing and programming teams. 
This tool also lets them automatically generate their [weekly Spotify playlist](https://open.spotify.com/playlist/2I5aVCoFDLMBBw49gT0xhf).

Tote_Bag_Mockup_1.webp.title: Frisse Gedachtes
Tote_Bag_Mockup_1.webp.description:
For Frisse Gedachtes, a student oranization for student wellbeing, I created a lot of promotional materials for their events and campaigns. This includes posters, social media posts, flyers, etc. 

The materials are often humorous and lighthearted, but they also aim to raise awareness about important issues such as mental health and wellbeing. 

We once got invited by [Stichting MIND](https://mindus.nl/) to an event to speak with several university and college presidents and other policymakers, and even the queen of the Netherlands, about the importance of student wellbeing and mental health. I was responsible for creating a presentation for this event, which was well received and led to some fruitful discussions. [NRC](https://www.nrc.nl/nieuws/2022/05/20/voor-studenten-komt-de-druk-van-alle-kanten-en-drank-en-drugs-ook-a4125091) wrote a not-so well-informed article on this wich I just [had to comment on](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/timzv_voor-studenten-komt-de-druk-van-alle-kanten-activity-6933552690501197824-IRlY?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAACsrL-IBSgsaWHoqoKV6ZAL9AbWcqFuHhk0).

The color of GIF.webp.title: In The Mood for a Vibe
The color of GIF.webp.description:
My MA thesis is about the mythical term 'vibes' and how Spotify turns vague concepts like “vibes” and “mood” into something measurable and marketable. By combining cultural analysis with data from Spotify’s API, I show how mood-based playlists are constructed through both language and algorithmic patterns. 

It argues that these playlists don’t just reflect emotions, but actively shape them, turning user feelings into data that can be categorized, predicted, and monetized. In doing so, Spotify positions itself as both a tastemaker and a system that subtly structures how we experience music and mood.

Synth.webp.title: Synth
Synth.webp.description:
I built a synthesizer/controller from scratch for my end-project at HKU Music & Technology. Both the code, hardware and body are built by me. 

In case you want to build something similar: 
It runs from a [Teensy](https://www.tinytronics.nl/shop/nl/teensy/main-boards/teensy-4.1) microcontroller. Some helpful resources: [this](https://www.reddit.com/r/arduino/comments/98bpnz/midi_controller_wiring_help/), [this](https://adam-meyer.com/arduino/CD74HC4067), [this](https://github.com/praashie/flatmate), [this](https://www.reddit.com/r/FL_Studio/comments/i8hwpq/guide_on_how_to_do_midiscripting/?solution=0548fb2849cc6e400548fb2849cc6e40&js_challenge=1&token=bbbe4bf1c9a2b5160829c4be34da58615578b469aa67d38e5e9b1895c5e0b1ae) and [this one](https://hackaday.com/2019/01/27/open-source-fader-bank-modulates-our-hearts/) helped me a lot. 

Design inspired by the incredible work of [Love Hultén](https://www.lovehulten.com/).
